response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an accomplished full-stack and web-systems engineer with strong TypeScript/Node.js credentials and solid software craftsmanship practices, but he is fundamentally misaligned with this role's core requirements. His C++ exposure amounts to roughly six months at National Instruments and a bachelor thesis project, both primarily on Linux rather than Windows — far short of the mandatory 5–10 years of hands-on Windows C++ development. He lists SQL generically but shows no evidence of SQL Server or Oracle depth, and his only financial-sector adjacency (RIDE Capital) involved TypeScript and GraphQL work on German retail investment tooling, which is not capital markets or fixed income. None of the desirable skills — Summit, STL, Python, Kafka/MQ — are evidenced in his profile.
strengths: Demonstrated software craftsmanship discipline: TDD, trunk-based development, CI/CD pipeline setup across multiple roles
Java experience (Altom Consulting, robotic arms project) satisfies one optional desirable skill
Broad polyglot background across embedded, web, and systems work shows adaptability and engineering range
concerns: C++ experience is minimal (~6 months, NI LabWindows role) and on Linux, not the Windows platform the role mandates for 5–10 years
No capital markets, fixed income, investment banking, or financial-product domain knowledge anywhere in the employment history
No evidence of SQL Server, Oracle, STL, Python, Kafka, MQ, or Summit — nearly all mandatory and desirable technical requirements are unmet

key_factors.factor: Insufficient C++ depth and wrong platform
No capital markets or fixed income domain exposure
Strong CI/CD and software craftsmanship practices
